decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.29°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective immediate action is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader material to enhance lateral heat dissipation away from the chip hotspot.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat spreading, reducing the local temperature gradient under the chip and lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if conductivity becomes too high relative to the base plate's extraction capacity
Further geometry changes (width/height) may be required if material tuning alone is insufficient
